findings.description: The HTML renders Part A as a single-page continuous layout rather than preserving the compact spreadsheet-style layout of the PDF. The table is stretched vertically with more whitespace between rows.
findings.evidence: PDF shows a compact table fitting on one page with tight row spacing; HTML shows expanded rows with significant padding, spreading content across more vertical space.

findings.description: The OMB NO. and REPORT PERIOD information positioning differs - in the PDF it appears in the upper right corner area, in the HTML it appears at the top left as smaller text above the title.
findings.evidence: PDF: OMB NO.: 0970-0234 appears upper right. HTML: appears as small text at top left above the title.

findings.description: The provision method columns ('x' markers) for line 22 Protective Services--Children show 'x' only under Public in both PDF and HTML, but the Private column is missing its 'x' in HTML - actually both match showing only Public x.
findings.evidence: Both PDF and HTML show 'x' under Public only for line 22. No discrepancy upon closer inspection.

findings.description: Row 32 in the PDF shows a two-line description within a single row; the HTML preserves this as a multi-line cell.
findings.evidence: PDF: 'SUM OF EXPENDITURES FOR SERVICES AND ADMINISTRATIVE COSTS' in row 32. HTML: Same text split across lines in the cell.

findings.description: The footnotes section at bottom of Part A (asterisk notes about block grants, sources, other services) is rendered differently - the HTML uses a separate styled section rather than inline text at the bottom of the table area.
findings.evidence: PDF shows footnotes directly below the table. HTML shows them in a separate bordered section below.

findings.description: Part B Recipients table header structure differs slightly. The PDF shows 'Adults' as a spanning header over multiple adult sub-columns, while HTML combines the header text differently.
findings.evidence: PDF: 'Adults' spans as a group header, with sub-headers 'Adults Age 59 Years & Younger', 'Adults Age 60 Years & Older', 'Adults of Unknown Age'. HTML: Headers read 'Adults Adults Age 59 Years & Younger' etc., duplicating the word 'Adults'.

findings.description: In Part B table headers, the word 'Adults' is duplicated in each sub-column header in the HTML, creating confusing headers like 'Adults Adults Age 59 Years & Younger' instead of just 'Adults Age 59 Years & Younger'.
findings.evidence: PDF headers: 'Adults Age 59 Years & Younger'. HTML headers: 'Adults Adults Age 59 Years & Younger'.

findings.description: The Attachment page content is preserved but rendered as a styled page with bullet points rather than the plain text list format in the PDF.
findings.evidence: PDF: Plain text list of fund sources. HTML: Bulleted list of fund sources.

findings.description: The 'Definition of Other Services' section on page 3 is rendered as a table in the HTML, matching the PDF's tabular layout with service names and descriptions.
findings.evidence: Both PDF and HTML present this as a two-column layout with service names on the left and descriptions on the right.

findings.description: Multiple h1 elements used in the HTML. The document has 'Part A. Expenditures and Provision Method' and 'Part B. Recipients' both as h1. Only one h1 should be used as the document title; the second should be h2 or lower.
findings.evidence:
HTML contains:

Part A. Expenditures and Provision Method

and

Part B. Recipients

. Both are top-level headings.

findings.description: In Part B Recipients table, Day Care--Children row shows 0 for Total in the HTML, but the PDF shows a blank/empty cell for that Total value. The PDF also shows the row with mostly blank cells or dashes rather than explicit zeros for some entries, though the HTML uses 0.
findings.evidence: PDF row 6 Day Care--Children shows blank cells for some values; HTML shows all zeros. This is a minor representational difference since blank likely means zero in this context.

findings.description: The page header/footer OMB information appears twice - once at the top of Part A section and once before Part B section, which mirrors the PDF's page-level headers. This is acceptable reformatting but noted.
findings.evidence: OMB NO.: 0970-0234 and EXPIRATION DATE: 11/30/2017 appear in two page-header divs.

findings.description: The HTML has nested <main> elements - a wrapping <main> in the body and another <main> inside it. While this is a structural HTML issue rather than content, it's technically invalid HTML structure.
findings.evidence: Outer <main> wraps inner <main> element containing the document content.
